description: Blind 8 guest data export for partner algorithm development. Only direct PII (names, phone, email, Instagram) removed. Name-based fields resolved to guest IDs.
fieldsTransformed: guests.referredBy → referredByGuestId (name resolved to guest ID, null if unresolved)
feedback.spendTimeWith → spendTimeWithGuestIds (comma-separated names resolved to guest ID array)

guests.screeningNotes: Product designer at a fintech startup downtown. Moved to Austin 6 months ago from San Francisco. Really into rock climbing — goes to ABP 3-4x/week. Said she's been struggling to build a social circle outside of work. Loves cooking Thai food and hosting people at her place. Very warm personality, laughed a lot on the call. Mentioned she's training for a half marathon in March.

Runs a small creative agency focused on branding for restaurants. Originally from San Antonio, been in Austin 4 years. Super into the food scene — knows chefs at several spots on Rainey. Also a climber, mentioned he goes to ABP on weekends. Plays guitar and goes to live music shows at least twice a week. Really thoughtful guy, asked a lot of questions about the community aspect of Blind 8.

Software engineer at Dell, been in Austin since college (UT grad). Grew up in Korea, moved to the US at 14. Big runner — she's done 3 marathons and is training for Boston. Volunteers at Austin Pets Alive on weekends. Mentioned she loves board games and has a huge collection. Kind of introverted at first but really opened up once we started talking about travel — she backpacked through Southeast Asia last summer.

High school music teacher in Round Rock. Plays piano, guitar, and drums. Side project producing beats — has a small but growing SoundCloud following. Recently got into cooking after watching a ton of YouTube — specifically into making pasta from scratch. Just got out of a long-term relationship and said he wants to put himself out there and meet new people. Really genuine energy on the call.

Pediatric resident at Dell Med. Originally from Houston, did undergrad at Rice. Works insane hours but said she needs something outside of medicine to keep her sane. Really into yoga and meditation — goes to Black Swan Yoga. Also mentioned she's trying to learn guitar. Loves spicy food and said she grew up cooking with her mom. Very bubbly and easy to talk to.

Freelance photographer, mostly does portraits and event work. Traveled through South America for a year — speaks decent Spanish. Just moved back to Austin after 2 years in Colombia. Really into film photography and has a darkroom setup at home. Also runs — did the Austin Marathon last year. Mentioned wanting to find a creative community here. Super chill, easy-going vibe.

Architect at a firm in East Austin, works on residential projects. Born and raised in Austin — parents own a Mexican restaurant on South 1st. Speaks fluent Spanish. Really into sustainability and green building. Volunteers with Habitat for Humanity. Also mentioned she does pottery on weekends at Feats of Clay. Said she loves hosting dinner parties at her place but wants to meet people outside her existing circle.

Data scientist at a health tech company, works remote. Moved from NYC 8 months ago. Really into specialty coffee — visits different roasters every weekend and is learning to roast his own beans. Plays pickup basketball at Ramsey Park. Also into board games and said he hosts game nights. Started learning pottery recently. Mentioned he misses the social density of NYC and is looking for ways to build deeper friendships here.
Works in real estate development. Into golf and tennis. Also a foodie — loves trying new restaurants. Recently started getting into wine. Pretty outgoing, seems like he'd gel well with the group.

Recent UT grad, works at a consulting firm. Into pilates and hiking. Said she's looking to build a social circle outside of college friends who all moved away. Very friendly and enthusiastic on the call.

guests.socialBravery: Omg let me tell y’all. I was in Ohio for a friends wedding and was staying in an Airbnb. All of the girls that were staying w me were flying in from different parts of the country and all their flights were delayed. Dinner rolled by and I was SO scared to go out by myself but I felt like I had to do it. I went to a local brewery and almost immediately I started chatting it up with a wedding crew. They were having a post dinner rehearsal night out. I ended up getting invited to that wedding. I obviously had my best friend’s wedding on the same day but it ended early enough for me to stop by the other random one. It was one of the best nights of my life. It was so much fun. We danced and drank all night long. Now I always try to force myself to do crazy things. They create the best memories :)

guests.socialBravery: Back when I hated my old job, I was feeling really emo and as one does, started binging American Idol (my long time guilty pleasure show). I’ve watched it so consistently since my parents loved that it has no language barrier. A week later I found out a few of my fav artists from the last season were playing a random concert in a Philly in some 200-person venue, so I decided to book an Amtrak over, snagged an airbnb, and spent my Black Friday watching a fantastic show. I was there alone, but befriended a pair of sisters in line. They were super cool and we hatched a plan to meet the artists after. We bought them shots, and somehow convinced them into going out with us?? Then we all got food, stayed up talking about life and playing music until 4 in the morning, and then I caught the train back to DC around 9. I’m actually not one to do much wild stuff, but sometimes I wonder what I’m missing out on. I’ve never made it out to a blind 8 signup, but I think it would remind me of that night.
